headline: Generic Medicine Adoption at Only 35% Despite 60% Cost Savings
context: Malaysian doctors prescribe branded medicines 65% of the time despite generics being equally effective and far cheaper.

cards.big: Generic medicine prescribing rate at 35% costs Malaysia RM3.2 billion extra annually
cards.sub: Identical active ingredients available at 40-60% lower cost are routinely bypassed

cards.big: NPRA approved 4,800 generic medicines but doctor preference favors branded alternatives
cards.sub: Pharmaceutical marketing budgets of RM1.4 billion annually influence prescribing habits

cards.big: Malaysia has no mandatory generic substitution law for pharmacists unlike 28 OECD countries
cards.sub: Pharmacists cannot switch prescriptions to cheaper equivalents without doctor approval

cards.big: India's generic medicine mandate through Jan Aushadhi stores saved patients RM12 billion since 2018
cards.sub: Government-backed generic pharmacies proved that quality and affordability coexist

cards.big: Prescribing expensive branded drugs when identical generics exist is a tax on patient trust
Should Malaysia mandate pharmacist generic substitution for all non-specialist prescriptions
